title: stemonoporus reticulatus
intro: Stemonoporus reticulatus, also known as the Malabar ironwood, is a species of tree in the family Stemonuraceae. It is native to the Western Ghats of India, particularly in the states of Karnataka and Kerala. The bark and leaves of the Malabar ironwood have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of the plant include reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and aiding in digestion, and herbal preparations such as decoctions and infusions are used to harness its medicinal properties.
